title: Replication of data using chunk identifiers
abstract: A data identifier for each data portion of a first group of different data portions of a first version of data is determined. The first version of the data is represented in a tree structure that references the determined data identifiers. A second version of the data is represented in a second tree structure using at least a portion of elements of the first tree structure of the first version. The second tree structure references one or more data identifiers of a portion of the second version of the data that is different from the first version of the data. The one or more data identifiers of the portion of the second version of the data that is different from the first version of the data are identified and sent. A response indicating which of the data portions corresponding to the sent one or more data identifiers are requested to be provided for replication is received.
claims:
Claims 1. A method of replicating data, comprising: determining a data identifier for each data portion of a first group of different data portions of a first version of data; representing the first version of the data in a tree structure that references the determined data identifiers; representing a second version of the data in a second tree structure using at least a portion of elements of the first tree structure of the first version, wherein the second tree structure references one or more data identifiers of a portion of the second version of the data that is different from the first version of the data; identifying and sending the one or more data identifiers of the portion of the second version of the data that is different from the first version of the data; and receiving a response indicating which of the data portions corresponding to the sent one or more data identifiers are requested to be provided for replication. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising sending the requested data portions. 3. The method of claim 2, further comprising: receiving an indication of an interruption associated with sending the requested data portions; determining a checkpoint associated with the sending the requested data portions; and resuming, based on the checkpoint, the sending the requested data portions. 4. The method of claim 2, wherein one or more nodes are configured to send in parallel different portions of the requested data portions. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein sending the one or more data identifiers of the portion of the second version of the data that is different from the first version of the data includes sending a corresponding data portion offset associated with each of the one or more data identifiers. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein sending the one or more data identifiers of the portion of the second version of the data that is different from the first version of the data includes sending a corresponding file offset associated with each of the one or more data identifiers. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein identifying the one or more data identifiers of the portion of the second version of the data that is different from the first version of the data includes traversing the second tree structure and the first tree structure. 8. The method of claim 7, further comprising determining one or more leaf nodes that are shared between the second tree structure and the first tree structure. 9. The method of claim 8, further comprising determining one or more leaf nodes that are not shared between the second tree structure and the first tree structure. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein a first node of the second tree structure corresponds to a modification to a particular data portion of the different data portions corresponding to a second node of the first tree structure. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the first node of the second tree structure is associated with a first data identifier of the modified particular data portion and the second node of the first tree structure is associated with a second data identifier of the particular data portion. 12. The method of claim 10, wherein the first node of the second tree structure is associated with a first data identifier of the modified particular data portion and a second data identifier associated with the first version of the particular data portion. 13. The method of claim 1, wherein at least one node of the second tree structure references at least one node of the first tree structure. 14. The method of claim 1, wherein the first tree structure comprises a root node, one or more intermediate nodes, and one or more leaf nodes. 15. The method of claim 1, wherein at least one of the one or more leaf nodes stores the one or more data identifiers of the portion of the second version of the data that is different from the first version of the data. 16. The method of claim 1, wherein a remote site is configured to receive the one or more data identifiers of the portion of the second version of the data that is different from the first version of the data and is configured to store a data portion that corresponds to at least one of the one or more data identifiers, wherein the data portion that corresponds to the at least one of the one or more data identifiers is associated with second data that is different than the data. 17. A system for replicating data, comprising: 

 a processor configured to: determine a data identifier for each data portion of a first group of different data portions of a first version of data; represent the first version of the data in a tree structure that references the determined data identifiers ; represent a second version of the data in a second tree structure using at least a portion of elements of the first tree structure of the first version, wherein the second tree structure references one or more data identifiers of a portion of the second version of the data that is different from the first version of the data; identify and send the one or more data identifiers of the portion of the second version of the data that is different from the first version of the data; and receive a response indicating which of the data portions corresponding to the sent one or more data identifiers are requested to be provided for replication; and a memory configured to store the data portion corresponding to at least one of the one or more identifiers. 18. The system of claim 17, wherein the processor is further configured to send the requested data portions. 19. The system of claim 17, wherein to identify the one or more data identifiers of the portion of the second version of the data that is different from the first version of the data, the processor is further configured to traverse the second tree structure. 20. A computer program product for replicating data, the computer program product being embodied in a non- transitory computer readable storage medium and comprising computer instructions for: determining a data identifier for each data portion of a first group of different data portions of a first version of data; representing the first version of the data in a tree structure that references the determined data identifiers; representing a second version of the data in a second tree structure using at least a portion of elements of the first tree structure of the first version, wherein the second tree structure references one or more data identifiers of a portion of the second version of the data 

 that is different from the first version of the data; identifying and sending the one or more data identifiers of the portion of the second version of the data that is different from the first version of the data; and receiving a response indicating which of the data portions corresponding to the sent one or more data identifiers are requested to be provided for replication.
